description: Prune boundary test was flaky — daysAgo(14) plus milliseconds of clock drift made age 14.0000001, passing the > 14 check
resolution: Math.floor the age-in-days computation so boundary records are treated as exactly N whole days old

content: No mocks. Tests use real filesystems — create temp dirs with mkdtemp, write real config/JSONL, assert against real file contents, clean up in afterEach.
Test file location mirrors src/ structure: test/commands/ for src/commands/, test/utils/ for src/utils/. Test file name matches source file name.

description: Used process.exit(1) in command handler. Tests hung or produced garbled output because exit killed the Vitest process.
resolution: Use process.exitCode = 1 instead. Lets the event loop drain and Vitest clean up.

description: Ajv schema had required and properties but no type: 'object'. Validation worked in dev but Ajv strict mode threw 'unknown keyword' in tests.
resolution: Always include type: 'object' alongside required and properties in every JSON schema definition/oneOf branch.

name: indirect-function-testing
description: Test unexported functions indirectly via observable outcomes. When internal functions like findCandidates are not exported, verify their behavior by testing the file system state, data structures, and other observable effects rather than calling them directly.

name: domain-isolation-testing
description: Verify domain filtering by testing record isolation across multiple domains. Create expertise files for different domains, add records to each, then verify that records remain segregated and that type-based grouping works correctly within each domain. This validates the foundation for domain-filtered operations.

name: scored-record-test-data
description: To test scoring sort in search tests: write ScoredRecord JSON directly to JSONL via appendFile (bypassing appendRecord type constraint). Use a separate domain (api) to avoid pre-existing records from beforeEach contaminating count assertions.

name: cli-json-mode-testing
description: To test JSON output mode of a CLI command that uses program.opts().json: (1) import registerXxxCommand, (2) create a Commander program with .option('--json', ...) and .exitOverride(), (3) spy on console.log/console.error, (4) chdir to tmpDir before parseAsync, (5) reset process.exitCode = 0 in afterEach to prevent exit-code pollution from error-path tests.

name: sync-command-testing
description: Testing sync command (git-integrated) requires: (1) process.chdir() to real git repo temp dirs, (2) spyOn console.log/console.error with mockImplementation to capture output, (3) git init + git config user/email + initial commit in beforeEach, (4) restore process.exitCode=0 in afterEach. JSON mode outputs to console.log (success) or console.error (error). Use program.exitOverride() to prevent process.exit during tests.

name: non-null-cast-replacement-playbook
description: To remove 'as Type' casts that substituted for former '!' non-null assertions in tests: (1) Array index + destructure with 'if (!x) throw'; (2) Inline '?.' matcher access for single reads; (3) Destructure + guard for threaded values like const {id} = r; if (!id) throw; (4) '.map(r => r.id as string)' becomes map with explicit check; (5) mock.calls in Bun are typed 'any' so redundant 'as string' can simply be removed. Distinguish from genuine casts: 'as const' literal narrowing, test fixture builders building Partial<T> into T via spread + cast — leave those alone.

name: snapshot-regression-via-pinned-time
description: Format-output regression test pins Date.now via setSystemTime so formatTimeAgo (which calls new Date()) produces stable strings; rendered output (markdown/compact/xml/plain), summaries, and IDs are written to test/utils/__snapshots__/*.json by setting UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1; subsequent runs assert byte-identical.

content: Inner describe blocks in test/commands/prime.test.ts that call process.chdir(tmpDir) MUST capture and restore cwd via per-block beforeEach/afterEach (let originalCwd: string; beforeEach(() => { originalCwd = process.cwd(); }); afterEach(() => { process.chdir(originalCwd); process.exitCode = 0; })). The outer afterEach only does rm(tmpDir) — it doesn't restore cwd. Without per-block restoration, the last test leaves process.cwd() pointing at a deleted tmpDir, which then breaks tests in OTHER files that use execSync('bun src/cli.ts ...') because Bun cannot resolve 'src/cli.ts' relative to a non-existent cwd. Symptom: completions/rank/doctor tests fail in the full suite but pass in isolation. Existing 'domain-not-found hint', 'manifest mode', and 'global --format flag' blocks already follow this pattern — copy from them when adding new describe blocks.

Worktree integration tests must realpath() the mkdtemp tmp dir before passing it to git init/git worktree add, because on macOS /tmp is a symlink to /private/tmp. resolveWorktreeRoot in src/utils/config.ts (via git rev-parse --git-common-dir) returns the resolved /private/tmp/... path, which won't equal the unresolved /tmp/... path the test compares against. Tests then silently pass for the wrong reason. Pattern: tmpDir = await realpath(await mkdtemp(...)).

name: runCliAsync-Bun-spawn
description: For true concurrent CLI integration tests (vs serial Bun.spawnSync), wrap Bun.spawn with a Promise.all that awaits proc.exited and reads stdout/stderr via new Response(proc.stdout/stderr).text(). Bun.spawnSync blocks the event loop and serializes spawns even inside Promise.all, defeating the concurrency the test is trying to exercise. See runCliAsync helper in test/commands/config.test.ts for the canonical shape.
